residual_risks: Milestone-6 through milestone-7 remain intentionally open and rows 6-7 stay NO in the mechanism table.

The accepted result freezes only the current closed primitive/runtime-binding set and eager sequencing contract; any broader primitive surface, FFI expansion, public lowering surface, or polymorphism-lowerability widening will need new evidence and a later accepted round.
evidence_summary: Reviewed the canonical round-226 worktree against master commit 5adb3702eb0d4e5230aa0a778f417075a20a6333, where HEAD, master, and merge-base all match and the live round exists as an uncommitted worktree diff. The implementation-owned merge payload stays limited to docs/architecture.md, docs/backend-native-pipeline.md, src/MLF/Backend/IR.hs, src/MLF/Backend/Convert.hs, src/MLF/Backend/LLVM/Lower.hs, docs/plans/2026-05-02-backend-ir-executable-boundary-mechanism-table.md, test/BackendLLVMSpec.hs, and test/RepoGuardSpec.hs, while orchestrator/state.json, the pointer stubs, and the active roadmap/round lineage artifacts are controller-owned activation and review bookkeeping. The round makes the closed primitive runtime-binding set and eager sequencing contract explicit across the synchronized contract surfaces, proves native nested __io_bind / __io_putStrLn execution in written order, flips only mechanism-table row 5 to YES while preserving rows 1-4 at YES and rows 6-7 at NO, passes the focused row-5 slices, and passes cabal build all && cabal test with 2343 examples and 0 failures. The inherited one-backend-IR, eager-runtime, no-lazy-STG, no-public-lower-IR, and no-fallback boundaries remain intact, and the round does not widen into milestone-6/7 claims, public lowering surfaces, or broad FFI expansion.
